metadata.title: CABINET:ContentRelevanceBasedNoiseReduction for Table Question Answering
organizations: Media and Data Science Research Lab, Adobe, India

sections.title: LLMs have been used for Table Understanding

figures.description: LLM table understanding tasks: QA, Fact Verification, and Dialogue examples

sections.title: Large Tabular Data degrades LLM Performance

figures.description: Bar charts showing accuracy drop as number of table cells increases across WikiTQ, FeTaQA, and WikiSQL

sections.title: Hard Decomposition can cause Information Loss

figures.description: Comparison between DATER decomposer and CABINET showing how relevance scoring prevents information loss

sections.title: Our Approach: CABINET assigns Relevance Score to Tabular Data Tokens through Two Modules - 1) Unsupervised Relevance Scorer and 2) Weakly Supervised Relevant Cell Highlighter

figures.description: CABINET model architecture showing URS and Cell Highlighter modules processing flattened tables

sections.title: CABINET (despite having less #params) outperforms several SoTA Methods

tables.description: Performance comparison table showing CABINET outperforming OmniTab, TAPEX, and DATER on WikiTQ, FeTaQA, and WikiSQL

sections.title: CABINET is more Robust to Noise and Table Perturbations

figures.description: Performance drop on WikiTQ under noise
Performance drop on FeTaQA under noise
Performance drop on WikiSQL under noise

sections.title: CABINET is more Performant while Handling Irrelevant Data in Large Tables

figures.description: Accuracy vs table size on WikiTQ comparing OmniTab and CABINET
S-BLEU vs table size on FeTaQA comparing OmniTab and CABINET
Accuracy vs table size on WikiSQL comparing ReasTAP and CABINET

sections.title: Clustering URS Output Representations is Helpful

figures.description: t-SNE visualization showing clear separation between relevant and non-relevant tokens in URS output

tables.description: Ablation study table showing the impact of different loss components (Clu, Sep, Sparse) on performance

sections.title: Using Both Modules gives Best Performance

tables.description: Table showing the impact of balancing unsupervised and cell highlighter modules using lambda weights
